items.title: Canberra paramedics become patients every couple of days

children.content: ACT Ambulance Service recorded more than 190 occupational violence incidents last financial year.

image.altText: Mr Dutton smiles in his uniform, standing in front of the logo for the ACT Emergency Services Agency.

items.title: ADFA cadet facing 79 extra charges after hidden camera found in bathroom

children.content: Police allege Tain Stephens was living at the Australian Defence Force Academy in Canberra when he hid a camera inside a soap dispenser in a female bathroom to capture intimate recordings of women.

image.altText: A man with short hair wearing a t-shirt walks out of a court building.

items.title: Year 11 and 12 students should be forced to study maths, review says

children.content: A committee has found students in the ACT should study maths through to the end of year 12 to ensure they have better long-term outcomes, arguing a lack of maths understanding can exacerbate social inequality.

gallery.altText: A generic photo of a student writing on a pad.

thumbnail.altText: A generic photo of a student writing on a pad.

avatar.alt: Niki Burnside is a journalist and digital producer based in Canberra.

items.title: ACT's school language education plan 'not fit for purpose', committee finds

children.content: The ACT government's blueprint to deliver language education in Canberra public schools is "not fit for purpose", a parliamentary committee finds, prompting calls for the government to once again deliver a centralised ATAR-level language learning plan.

image.altText: Students look at a whiteboard with a mixture of English and Japanese writing on it, as a teacher stands nearby.

items.title: 'Terrifying' roadside assistance ordeal sparks national change

children.content: A woman's campaign for change after her own frightening experience being broken down outside Canberra will see NRMA roadside assistance contractors required to provide a code to customers to verify their identity.

image.altText: A woman with shoulder-length brown hair and glasses stands in front of a road with a phone in her hand.

items.title: 'Towelling the bloke up in the cells': Shocking police emails revealed

children.content: Emails reveal an extraordinary exchange between AFP officers after an Indigenous man was allegedly beaten and illegally strip-searched at a Canberra watchhouse.

image.altText: A person's face and police tape and bars in a graphic image.

items.title: 'Never trust a label': Mystery violin willed to Canberra Youth Orchestra

children.content: A violin gifted to not-for-profit organisation Music for Canberra bears the hallmarks of early 18th century European style. But one expert says only forensic testing can prove its true origins.

gallery.altText: A man with short black hair plays the violin.

thumbnail.altText: A man with short black hair plays the violin.

items.title: Delay in Telstra informing government of outage, as Liberal stands by Triple Zero test

children.content: Telstra has defended its communication process, insisting the government and other stakeholders were informed "very, very early" in the morning.

articles.title: Broken timers trigger nationwide Telstra outage

children.content: Telstra says Wednesday's nationwide outage was caused by malfunctioning "nodes" that manage time synchronisation within some of the network data centres, but has no idea what caused the timer issues.

image.altText: A close up of a Telstra logo on the side of a pay phone

gallery.altText: A brown-haired woman wearing a navy blazer, looking solemn.

items.title: How the Telstra outage impacted these Australians

image.altText: Two women hugging holding flowers.

children.content: Millions have potentially been impacted by the Telstra outage. Here's how it affected Australians doing basic everyday things, from making calls to measuring blood sugar levels.

items.title: Trump says Iran ceasefire 'over' after latest strikes

children.content: Donald Trump says the ceasefire with Iran is over, warning that the US would likely engage in additional strikes, following fresh attacks across the Gulf.

items.title: Cleary 'silences the critics' as NSW make Origin history with decider win

children.content: NSW has produced a historic performance to win the State of Origin series with a win over Queensland at Lang Park in Brisbane.

articles.title: New South Wales writes a new fate in blood after epic decider

children.content: Against all odds and with most of their state doubting them, Laurie Daley's New South Wales side has pulled off one of the greatest wins in Blues history.

image.altText: A rugby league player in blue holds up his hands in celebration

articles.title: Quick hits: Arrow's standing 'O' and Queensland 'ripped off'

children.content: Jai Arrow is the only Queenslander to touch the State of Origin trophy as the Blues dominate the decider, but a NSW great questions a pivotal try. Here are your quick hits from the State of Origin decider.

items.title: Native title holders celebrate return of 'special country' in access win

items.title: SA confirms second H5 bird flu case, two more suspected in Perth and WA

items.title: Court told former MP knew lying was 'part and parcel' of visa scheme

children.content: A court hears former Wagga Wagga MP Daryl Maguire knew being dishonest to the Department of Immigration was a component of an alleged fraudulent visa scheme.

image.altText: A man in a dark suit, white shirt and tie looks towards the camera in an urban environment.

items.title: This Place: Djimubarn, sacred rock art shelter

children.content: In Western Arnhem Land the Markani estate of the Madjawarr clan is full of stories.
As part of NAIDOC Week the ABC is looking at Indigenous peoples' connection to country, and what makes it so special.
NAIDOC Week is marking 50 years of Deadly and runs from July 5 until July 12.

items.title: A bus, a solar-powered stage and a journey across remote Australia

articles.title: 'Oldest continually practised medicine' being passed on to students

children.content: Aboriginal Australians have used native plants as bush medicine for tens of thousands of years. On Larrakia land, medical students are learning how nature can heal.

items.title: The faces in the crowd at the Ayatollah's funeral
